signals.evidence: 'John Deere is evaluating a second vendor - for intake, sourcing and spend - will complete eval in 2 months - check in on July 20th' (SF task); Next_Step adds the POC 'will not move forward' and frames ramifications as 'on hold, in-house build, or competitive loss'.
Eduardo Viera (Global Director, primary sponsor) replied by email: 'We are evaluating options and Zycus is one of those. Karen will be sharing more details in your next call.'
Karen Pagorek 'suggested a touch base in 2 months - and has accepted a 20th July Connect'; calendar event 'Arthur (Zycus) and Karen Touch Base' plus task 'Clarify Call - follow up on evaluation'.
CloseDate pushed 30 Sep 2026 to 22 Feb 2027 (field history), one week after the 4 May push from 26 Jun to 30 Sep; third push of 2026 (27 Mar to 26 Jun on 26 Feb).
'Delivered POC Details to Karen and Team on 31st March... POC for ANA and Spend was positive - need to connect on collab for Agentic Strat Sourcing' (SF Next_Step__c, April tracking).

Karen Pagorek attended the Zycus Agentic AI Procurement summit during the pause ('It was great to see that you attended our Agentic AI Procurement summit last week', email 21 May 2026), a warm counter-signal to the 11 May pause.
Could-not-connect outbound calls: Gia Duke twice (3 Jun) and Kathleen Johnson (2 Jul, Horizon call step 1); the 11 Jun agent-sprawl blogs email to Karen drew no reply.

Karen Pagorek email stepping back from the proof-of-value path: 'As part of our current planning, we're reviewing and assessing a range of alt...' (truncated in the activity log); Arthur's 5 May reply records his disappointment 'that we could not jointly advance the evaluation agenda'.

top_rationale: The defining signal: John Deere paused the Zycus POC on 11 May 2026 and is deep-diving an unnamed second I/O vendor covering intake, sourcing and spend, on a self-set two-month window ending at the 20 Jul check-in.

Zycus is confirmed still in the race: Eduardo Viera, the sponsor who funded the program on hard savings, replied on 12 Jun that 'Zycus is one of those' options and that Karen will share details at the next call.
The only live milestone is buyer-owned: Karen Pagorek suggested the touch base herself and accepted the 20 Jul meeting where the rival-evaluation outcome lands.

Value proof is the open wound: April feedback on the ANA and Spend POC was positive, but the buyer stalled at 'quantify incremental value' after challenging the December ROI claims as 'pretty optimistic'.
Thread health is thin: beyond Karen, June-July outreach to Gia Duke and Kathleen Johnson could not connect, the blogs email went unanswered, and CFO Joshua Jepsen has never engaged.
Close credibility is eroding: the date slipped three times in 2026 (27 Mar to 22 Feb 2027) and the deal has sat 216 days in Shortlisted.

drivers: ⚠️ The buyer paused the Zycus POC on 11 May 2026 to deep-dive an unnamed rival across intake, sourcing and spend, and Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun email confirms a live multi-option evaluation in which Zycus is not stated as leading. ► Walk into the 20 Jul call with the positive ANA/Spend POC results and a rival counter-brief to reclaim the front-runner slot.

⚠️ The ROI case is unproven in Deere's own numbers: Karen Pagorek called the claimed gains 'pretty optimistic' (18 Dec 2025), the 70-to-80-data-point benchmark exercise never ran, and the deal stalled at 'quantify incremental value'. ► Send the benchmark-and-reference pre-read before 20 Jul and propose the value-engineering exercise as the restart step.

✅ The economic-buyer door is open: Eduardo Viera funded the program on hard savings ('last year in '24, we secured funding', 30 Sep 2025), set a complement-not-replace frame, and replied personally on 12 Jun that Zycus remains an option.

✅ Agentic differentiation has landed: AIS 6.0 Strong, Karen Pagorek co-designed three AI pilots and pressure-tested autonomous-sourcing definitions (18 Dec 2025), April tracking logged 'POC for ANA and Spend was positive', and she attended the Agentic AI summit mid-pause (13 May 2026).

⚠️ Power coverage is one cooling thread: Karen Pagorek is the only active contact, CFO Joshua Jepsen has never engaged, and June-July calls to Gia Duke and Kathleen Johnson could not connect. ► Convert Eduardo's reply into a post-call make-versus-buy session and land Horizon invites to rebuild second and third threads.

⚠️ Close credibility is thin: the date was pushed three times in 2026 (27 Mar to 26 Jun to 30 Sep to 22 Feb 2027) with 216 days in Shortlisted. ► Rebuild a dated mutual plan on the 20 Jul call and hold the forecast at Pipeline until the evaluation restarts.
win.focus_now: Win the 20 Jul re-engagement call: pair proven pilot value with a rival counter-brief and convert Karen's check-in into a restarted evaluation with Eduardo Viera back in the room.
win.ceiling_applied: Shortlisted (RFP-round) ceiling 60; recorded stage held, not binding at 37.

drivers: ⚠️ The buyer imposed a standoff on 11 May 2026: the POC is paused, Karen Pagorek asked Zycus to wait two months while a rival is deep-dived, and there is no buyer-side forward action in the last 14 days. ► Hold the 20 Jul call and leave it with a dated restart plan, not another check-in.

⚠️ The close date was pushed twice within a week (4 May: 26 Jun to 30 Sep; 11 May: to 22 Feb 2027), the third push of 2026. ► Re-baseline the close plan on the 20 Jul outcome and re-forecast honestly.
✅ A buyer-confirmed future milestone stands: Karen Pagorek suggested the touch base herself and accepted the 20 Jul meeting, where she will share the evaluation details Eduardo promised.
✅ Cross-channel buyer pulse exists inside 30 days: Eduardo Viera replied on 12 Jun that Zycus is still one of the options, and Karen attended the Zycus Agentic AI summit on 13 May during the pause.

⚠️ All other outreach is one-way: calls to Gia Duke (3 Jun, twice) and Kathleen Johnson (2 Jul) could not connect and the 11 Jun agentic-blogs email drew no reply. ► Route re-engagement through the accepted 20 Jul call and Horizon invitations instead of cold dials.

⚠️ The customer rarely initiates: outside the 11 May pause message and the 12 Jun reply, Zycus drives every touch. ► On 20 Jul, ask Karen to name the buyer's own next step and its date as the test of real re-engagement.
mom.focus_now: Protect and win the buyer-accepted 20 Jul call; it is the only live milestone between a restarted evaluation and a drift to the rival or an in-house build.

waiting_on_buyer.item: Second-vendor evaluation outcome and re-engagement decision: Karen Pagorek to share the assessment details (including which vendor and what happens to the paused ANA/Spend/iSource pilots) on the 20 Jul call, as Eduardo Viera committed on 12 Jun

best_practices.item: Build and send the 20 Jul proof pack: AQPC/Hackett benchmark comparison, a Tenneco-style manufacturing reference offer, and the positive April ANA and Spend POC results in one pre-read
best_practices.rank_reason: Buyer doubt about Zycus's claims is the active deal-killer: Karen called the gains 'pretty optimistic' (18 Dec 2025) and the POC stalled at 'quantify incremental value' (11 May 2026); credibility building leads

best_practices.item: Prepare the competitive counter for the 20 Jul call: identify the unnamed I/O vendor, script differentiation questions, and position every pilot as accelerating the iProcure roadmap rather than replacing it
best_practices.rank_reason: An unnamed rival holds the buyer's exclusive attention until 20 Jul, and Deere's build culture plus SAP IT resistance (30 Sep 2025) make do-nothing the standing fallback

best_practices.item: Propose the Cary onsite working session for the week after the 20 Jul call while Arthur is in the Charlotte area
best_practices.rank_reason: Face time has lapsed since December and Next_Step already flags the Charlotte window (11 May 2026); an onsite compresses the restart cycle

best_practices.item: Re-thread power after the call: convert Eduardo Viera's 12 Jun reply into a phased make-versus-buy session and open a route toward CFO Joshua Jepsen's office via Horizon invitations to Kathleen Johnson and Gia Duke
best_practices.rank_reason: Single-threaded through a cooling evaluator; the funding owner is responsive but passive and the CFO has never engaged

empty_section_notes.prospect_requirements: Nothing pending: no unaddressed buyer asks are open; the one live buyer-initiated step, the two-month check-in Karen requested on 11 May, is already scheduled and accepted for 20 Jul.
empty_section_notes.zycus_commitments: Nothing owed: no live Zycus on-call commitments are open; the December 2025 proposal artifacts were overtaken by the March POV package and the 11 May pause, and nothing new has been promised since.
